title: Bare Bones PBR x128
description: Bare Bones PBR with x128 textures
body:
![Bare Bones PBR x128 Logo](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bf389aab887379aa10aed90b44f59d08472edfec.png)
-----------

<details>
<summary>Video 1</summary>

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/z7E5M5oTc18" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

</details>

<details>
<summary>Video 2</summary>

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/Sd_myKDQPsI" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

</details>



I am using IterationRP shader in the screenshots

_BSL Shader works fine, the preset is below_

In most shaders you can use "PBR only" settings instead of "hardcoded" to get the right emissive effect on blocks. As example with hardcoded enabled, torches and campfires will be glowing (even the wood part) whereas with "PBR only" the fire part only will glow

⛏️You have to place the resource pack **above** the default [Bare Bones](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/bare-bones) in the list

> *The default Bare Bones is needed to get textures of items, chests or minecarts*

- What's the difference with [Bare Bones Simple PBR](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/bare-bones-simple-pbr) ?

It is x128 instead of x256, which improve performance (I was running at 120fps with Simple PBR, 155fps with this resource pack, that can be very useful for low end computers or modpacks)

-----------

### [Trailer Vibes Modpack](https://modrinth.com/modpack/trailer-vibes) ✨

-----------

**Shader settings :**


|iterationRP 0.8.22 |[BSL](https://modrinth.com/shader/bsl-shaders)  |
|-------------|-------- |
|[.txt](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1zBSAM3NHCoW-ILED4FYh3ToK5ofbmWly/view?usp=sharing)         |[.txt](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1fcuphxbLW_lBJ_u8v-fT_sFfEl08nbvp/view?usp=drive_link)    |
|[shaders.properties](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1h20bTUAQed2TzkvVYRM4VYJQS8BiCEjL/view?usp=sharing)      |  |
|[block.properties](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LfxWEzu3iSnFeUSNn0bea_r9ggxY-j5h/view?usp=sharing)       |         |

- **.txt** the shader settings to get the same lighting as the pictures, to place inside the shaderpacks folder

- **shader.properties** to activate Vanilla clouds, you have to place it inside the shader itself

- **block.properties** to remove subsurface scattering on leaves and vines, to place inside the shader as well
